intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as distinct, healthy specimens. Only P3 (Pothos) shows evidence of existence via the white rabbit anchor and associated soil debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos remains, though obscured by low light; white rabbit anchor confirmed.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.

plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen present, structural integrity appears stable but dormant.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2/P3 area, no visible plant matter.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of cracking.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell or mineral deposits) in the P3 pot area.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, there has been zero vegetative progression. The environment appears static, with no new leaf emergence or growth. The lighting transition in the final image suggests a shift to a lower-spectrum or night-mode capture, further obscuring detail.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe neglect or dormancy. The lack of visible foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests these specimens have either been removed or have suffered total senescence. P3 is the only surviving occupant, though it is currently in a non-thriving, low-light state.
vision_report.anomalies: The white rabbit anchor is the only consistent landmark. The 'New Introduction' of white debris (eggshells) in the P3 pot is the only notable change in the environment.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided sequence. My first action was to establish the baseline against the registry, followed by a frame-by-frame comparison to track growth. I validate that the registry is currently inaccurate, as the majority of the plants are missing or dead. The environment is currently static and shows no signs of active botanical life outside of the P3 Pothos, which is struggling in the current lighting conditions.
